metric_name: Unicenter TNG annual revenue run rate FY1997
metric_value: $1 billion annual run rate (industry-leading)
notes: Kastner compares this to 'Microsoft's heralded NT Server' financial success; 7,000+ customers, 200+ ISV partners, 37 OS platforms.

metric_value: 7,000+ customers
notes: Supporting 200+ ISV partners and products running on 37 different operating system platforms.

metric_value: 10 minutes to install on NT server and discover all enterprise devices (Aberdeen's own installation)
notes: Aberdeen Group installed Framework on its own NT server, discovering desktops, hubs, routers, internet gateways, and recently installed Network Computers in 10 minutes.

metric_value: Identifies all network devices including IP devices for virtual network connections
notes: Automatic network discovery without manual configuration; key differentiator vs. Tivoli TME's manual approach.

metric_value: Library of all collected device information including version, release category, planning/upgrade/expansion properties
notes: Kastner calls the repository 'an under-appreciated storehouse of network asset and management process information.'

metric_value: 2D geographic topology maps + 3D virtual-reality floor plan maps showing physical device locations within buildings
notes: 3D mapping was industry-first graphical enterprise management feature; Aberdeen considered 'hands down, the best enterprise management user interface available anywhere.'

metric_value: Stores policies/operational thresholds; flags unusual conditions; updates repository as configurations change; periodic virus scanning
notes: Enables proactive management; example: 'log full' condition monitoring across multiple servers to single console.

metric_value: Calendar-based job scheduling for cross-platform automated operations
notes: Kastner cites scheduling as one of Unicenter's 'renowned' capabilities integrated into the Framework.

metric_value: Active server-based scanning at email/attachment entry point; monitors shared folders
notes: Anti-virus built into base Framework; unusual to include in 1997 enterprise management baseline.

metric_value: Razor/razor-blade model: give away Framework (razor) with server hardware; sell Unicenter TNG upgrades (razor blades) to mass market
notes: Kastner's explicit 'razor and razor-blades' analogy; CA distributing 20,000 free CDs at CA World; nine hardware partners bundling Framework with servers.

metric_value: More than 1.5 million copies of Framework to ship with systems in next year per CA estimates
notes: CA estimates from CA World 97 conference; nine household-name server hardware partners committed to bundling.

metric_value: Serious competitor with large installed base but has 'clearly lost momentum'; HP has not decided whether to reinvigorate OpenView product development
notes: HP's own systems division endorsed Unicenter Framework as 'preferred' management platform, undermining HP OpenView.

metric_value: CA's most obvious competitor; TME requires remote-network configuration estimates and 'days spent in network and asset evaluation' vs. CA's 10-minute Framework setup
notes: Aberdeen positions CA Framework's 10-minute install as major advantage over Tivoli's complex setup.

metric_value: Sun capably supports Solstice customers but 'more interested in partnering for future technology than building own new products'
notes: Kastner sees Sun as declining competitor; Sun acquired by Oracle in 2010.

metric_value: After difficult start-up phase, OpenVision sold earlier in 1997; acquired by Veritas Software
notes: Kastner reports OpenVision's sale as example of competitor consolidation; acquired by Veritas Software 1997.

metric_value: Framework will become de facto enterprise management standard; Unicenter TNG will earn industry leadership position and competitors' respect
notes: Aberdeen positions Framework as 'enterprise management entry-level user choice and de facto ISV platform choice.'

metric_name: Framework de facto standard actual outcome
metric_value: CA revenues grew to $6.1B by 2000; Unicenter reached one-quarter of overall revenue; however CA faced major accounting fraud scandal 2004-2006; HP OpenView not permanently displaced; Tivoli remained competitive; CA acquired by Broadcom 2018 for $18.9B
notes: Source: NYT April 2001; CA Wikipedia; Broadcom acquisition 2018. CA achieved growth but accounting scandal undermined market leadership; Unicenter did not become de facto standard.

metric_name: Competitive consolidation prediction from Framework strategy
metric_value: Framework's aggressive merchandising will result in competitive consolidation; fewer but stronger choices will make IT and ISV decision-making easier
notes: Kastner predicts market consolidation around CA/Unicenter and one or two others.

metric_name: Enterprise management competitive consolidation actual outcome
metric_value: Market consolidated but around IBM Tivoli, HP Operations Manager (OpenView successor), and CA; Microsoft also entered with SCOM; open-source Nagios/Prometheus disrupted by 2010
notes: Consolidation occurred but CA did not achieve the dominant position Kastner predicted; IBM Tivoli remained stronger than Aberdeen suggested.

metric_name: IS administrator target audience for Framework
metric_value: Those deploying distributed systems at 1000 bank branches will 'cry for joy' at Framework's easy installation and self-discovery
notes: Kastner uses vivid language; specifically calls out multi-branch financial services as ideal Framework use case.

metric_name: CA company size and position at time of study
metric_value: Third-largest independent software company; $4B+ annual revenue; revenues grew from ~$1.2B to $4B in seven years
notes: Source: CNET July 1997 'CA ascends to big leagues': 'catapulted itself to the position of third-largest independent software company in the world.'

metric_name: SAP AG as Framework ISV partner
metric_value: CA actively lining up SAP AG to use Framework as application management base
notes: SAP R/3 management was an optional Unicenter TNG Advanced Option; Framework partnership would extend SAP management capabilities to ISV ecosystem.

metric_name: Framework discovery speed advantage vs. Tivoli
metric_value: CA Framework enables typical IS administrators to configure enterprise management in minutes from CD; vs. Tivoli's days for network and asset evaluation
notes: Aberdeen's own 10-minute installation confirms this benchmark claim.

metric_name: CA acquisition of DEC Polycenter products
metric_value: CA acquired Digital Equipment's Polycenter management products and tightly integrated them with CA management console
notes: DEC Polycenter integration demonstrated CA's commitment to maintaining market leadership through acquisition strategy.

metric_name: CA total annual revenue at time of announcement
metric_value: $4+ billion annual revenue (Unicenter TNG at $1B run rate = ~25% of total)
notes: Kastner notes Unicenter TNG revenues grew to $1B in 3 years — 'a fast start on the same financial order of success as Microsoft's heralded NT Server.'
